authors_affiliation: Andrew Demonbreun, Grace M Mirsky (Benedictine University, Lisle, IL, USA)

result.evaluation_split: official hidden full test set (abstract: validation score 0.214, full test score 0.205, rank 20/41)

self_reported_metrics.other: Unofficial phase best entry: F_beta = 0.310, G_beta = 0.170. Training validation accuracy ranged 83-98% across different arrhythmias (no single aggregate figure reported).

model.architecture_details: Twelve independent per-lead models, each a modified SqueezeNet CNN (transfer learning) fine-tuned on scalogram images generated from that lead's ECG signal via continuous wavelet transform; final six SqueezeNet layers modified (60% dropout, 1x1 conv with filters = number of output classes, classification layer without preset labels); predictions from the 12 lead-specific models combined via a voting scheme (each model contributes 0.083 per diagnosis code; codes with summed score >0.3 (>=4 votes) are assigned, else the max-scoring code(s) are used).
model.key_novelty: Trains a separate SqueezeNet transfer-learning CNN per ECG lead on wavelet scalogram images (rather than one model over all 12 leads), then fuses the 12 lead-level predictions with a simple vote-counting scheme to allow multi-label diagnosis with increased confidence.

input.leads_used: 12 (each lead modeled independently, then fused by voting)
input.preprocessing: ECG signals converted to scalograms (time-frequency representation of absolute continuous wavelet transform coefficients) per lead, used as images for SqueezeNet transfer learning

data.training_datasets: Official PhysioNet/CinC 2020 Challenge training data (dataset breakdown not itemized in paper); diagnosis categories reduced to the 27 individually scored classes plus 49 label combinations occurring at least 50 times in the training set (76 total diagnosis categories); records without at least one of these 76 categories were excluded
data.class_imbalance_handling: Balanced subsampling: only ten example signals per diagnosis combination were used for training, chosen for a balanced dataset processable within limited computing resources

training.loss_function: crossentropyex (cross-entropy) classification loss (default SqueezeNet output layer)
training.optimizer: Stochastic gradient descent with momentum (SGDM); initial learning rate 3e-4, mini-batch size 10, max epochs 15

contribution.summary: Applies wavelet-scalogram transfer learning with a modified SqueezeNet to build 12 separate per-lead ECG classifiers and combines their outputs with a vote-counting scheme, enabling multi-label arrhythmia diagnosis while leveraging lead-specific anatomical sensitivity to different arrhythmias.
